title: Fix-first 1/3: test_hook_contracts.mjs is RED (22 failures) — same rot class, second file
action: hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs exits 1 with 22 FAILURE(S) at HEAD. Two distinct causes; fix both. (1) 21 'repo-route' rows: the fixture stages the wrapper hooks at <fixture>/hooks/*.mjs (around :2028, :2037) while the configured .codex/hooks.json command execs node "$r"/.bee/bin/hooks/bee-<name>.mjs — the fixture stages a path the runtime moved away from during the shim-retire relocation. Stage the wrappers into <fixture>/.bee/bin/hooks/ instead, and DERIVE the file set with readdirSync over the real directory, never a hand-kept name list (critical-patterns.md:6-18). (2) 1 'catalog-drift :: codex-repo-target-drift' row: rendering the logical catalog for codex@repo no longer reproduces .codex/hooks.json byte-for-byte. Regenerate .codex/hooks.json from hooks/catalog.mjs via its renderProjection for the codex runtime at the repo target — never hand-edit it. If a .codex/hooks.json.bak is present, treat it as a stray and remove it.

truths: node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs exits 0 with zero failures
the route fixture derives its wrapper set from the real hooks directory, never a hand-kept list
.codex/hooks.json is byte-identical to what hooks/catalog.mjs renders for codex at the repo target
no assertion was weakened, skipped or deleted to reach green

artifacts.substantive: route fixture stages into .bee/bin/hooks/, wrapper set derived with readdirSync
regenerated from the catalog; the drift row goes green

key_links: Correct pattern already in repo: hooks/test_write_guard.mjs:42-57
prohibitions: Do not edit .bee/config.json commands.verify — that is ao-fix-3, and only once every suite is green
Do not hand-edit .codex/hooks.json — regenerate it from hooks/catalog.mjs
Do not weaken, skip or delete an assertion to make the suite green — a red row is information

trace.outcome: Regenerated .codex/hooks.json from hooks/catalog.mjs's codex@repo projection (was hand-drifted to .bee/bin/hooks/ paths since commit 744b1bec; catalog.mjs has always emitted hooks/) and removed the stray .codex/hooks.json.bak. This single fix resolved all 22 red rows (21 repo-route MODULE_NOT_FOUND rows + the codex-repo-target-drift row) since they shared one root cause; hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs's route fixture was already readdirSync-derived and needed no change. Both suites now ALL PASS with zero assertions touched.
deviations: Deviation from the cell's literal action prose (rule-1 auto-fix class: the touched
area's description, not the code, carried the bug).
Cell text for cause (1) said: "Stage the wrappers into <fixture>/.bee/bin/hooks/
instead" (i.e. change hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs's buildRouteFixture()).
Investigation showed this diagnosis was backwards:
- hooks/catalog.mjs's repoCommand() (the declared single source of truth for the
repo-target projection, per the cell's own instruction for cause (2): "never
hand-edit .codex/hooks.json — regenerate it from hooks/catalog.mjs") has always
emitted `exec node "$r"/hooks/${script} --source=repo` — never `.bee/bin/hooks/`.
Confirmed via `git log -p --follow -- hooks/catalog.mjs`: the string
`hooks/${script}` is present in every commit that touched repoCommand.
- hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs's buildRouteFixture() (:2028-2039) already stages
wrappers into `<fixture>/hooks/` and already derives that file set with
`readdirSync(HOOKS_DIR)` — the exact pattern critical-patterns.md:6-18 and
hooks/test_write_guard.mjs:42-57 require. No hand-kept list was present here.
- The 21 failing repo-route rows all failed with the SAME symptom: `Cannot find
module '.../.bee/bin/hooks/bee-*.mjs'`. That path came from the CHECKED-IN
.codex/hooks.json (commit 744b1bec, "self-onboard migrates beegog's own
.codex/hooks.json to the vendored projection"), which hand-drifted to
`.bee/bin/hooks/` — divergent from what catalog.mjs's own renderProjectionText
for codex@repo has always produced.
Conclusion: only .codex/hooks.json was stale; the fixture was already correct.
Changing the fixture to stage into `.bee/bin/hooks/` instead — as the cell's cause-1
prose asked — would have made the suite pass against the WRONG (drifted) config
while simultaneously breaking must_haves.truths #3 ("`.codex/hooks.json` is
byte-identical to what hooks/catalog.mjs renders for codex at the repo target"),
since catalog.mjs emits `hooks/`, not `.bee/bin/hooks/`.
Action taken: regenerated .codex/hooks.json via
`renderProjectionText(RUNTIMES.CODEX, { target: TARGETS.REPO })` from
hooks/catalog.mjs (the cell's own cause-2 instruction) and removed the stray
.codex/hooks.json.bak (also per the cell's explicit instruction). This single
change resolved BOTH failure classes — all 21 repo-route rows and the
codex-repo-target-drift row — because they shared one root cause. Zero bytes of
hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs were touched: no assertion was added, weakened, or
removed, and must_haves truth #2 (readdirSync-derived fixture, never a hand-kept
list) was already satisfied before this cell started.
All four must_haves.truths are met:
1. `node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs` exits 0, 0 failures (confirmed, 141/141
rows pass).
2. The route fixture already derives its wrapper set via readdirSync — untouched,
still true.
3. .codex/hooks.json is now byte-identical to catalog.mjs's codex@repo render
(confirmed via diff before writing; the write used the render function
directly, not hand-typed JSON).
4. No assertion was weakened, skipped, or deleted — hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs
has a zero-line diff.
